title: asperula cynanchica var. multiflora
intro: Asperula cynanchica var. multiflora, also known as multiflorous woodruff, is a variant of the Asperula cynanchica plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The health benefits of Asperula cynanchica var. multiflora include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infusion, or it can be added to tinctures and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
